name: Stansberry Research
subject: Is Your Portfolio Caught in A “Post-Election Trap”?
body:
One of my most senior analysts has been sending this around – and it's startling.

# Is Your Portfolio Caught in A "Post-Election Trap"?
* * *
Most investors don't see [the problem](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/fL7ejAiV_WRcOc4QwuzcOQ~~/AARclAA~/RgRpTCFCP0SSaHR0cHM6Ly90cmFja2luZy5jbGlja3N0cmlwZTEuY29tL2FmZl9jP29mZmVyX2lkPTIxMzkmYWZmX2lkPTEwOTQmc291cmNlPUVtYWlsJnN1YjE9e3RyYW5zYWN0aW9uX2lkfSZzdWIyPXBvcDEyMDEyNGEmc3ViMz02NDgmc3ViND1STS1VQ04tMjAyNDEyMjNXA3NwY0IKZ2VCjWlnsAt91FIUZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb21YBAAAAAA~) yet... and many think this current rally could keep going for months or even years.
But one of my most senior analysts has been sending this around - and it's startling.
The last time the country looked like it does today, the "rally" at the time didn't keep going at all.
In fact, the market abruptly crashed...
But that was just the beginning of the problem.
Next...
Stocks proceeded to go nowhere for 16 years. And that _still_ wasn't the worst part...
The worst part was, by the end, many investors lost 80% of their biggest positions in real terms.
That number is hard to imagine... but this really happened - at a time in American history that looks eerily similar to today.
I can't possibly explain this all in a single e-mail.
[So it's best you go hear it directly from senior analyst Dan Ferris](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/fL7ejAiV_WRcOc4QwuzcOQ~~/AARclAA~/RgRpTCFCP0SSaHR0cHM6Ly90cmFja2luZy5jbGlja3N0cmlwZTEuY29tL2FmZl9jP29mZmVyX2lkPTIxMzkmYWZmX2lkPTEwOTQmc291cmNlPUVtYWlsJnN1YjE9e3RyYW5zYWN0aW9uX2lkfSZzdWIyPXBvcDEyMDEyNGEmc3ViMz02NDgmc3ViND1STS1VQ04tMjAyNDEyMjNXA3NwY0IKZ2VCjWlnsAt91FIUZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb21YBAAAAAA~).
Dan once wrote to an entire list of people and told them one of the world's biggest banks was about to collapse. He wrote these exact words:
_"These guys are hiding something. I expect a wipeout."_
It was March 2008, and he was writing about Lehman Brothers.
Few wanted to hear it.
But Dan was right about Lehman and the mortgage crisis. Lehman collapsed 5 months later.
And Dan has been right about nearly every bubble of the last 25 years including the COVID crash, the collapse of crypto in 2017, and the end of the Nasdaq bubble in 2021.
That's why Dan's work is followed by employees at banks like Wells Fargo, Barclays, and UBS. And it's why he has been featured in publications like _Barron's_ and Bloomberg.
Now that the election is over... as he puts it:
"_Millions are about to be blindsided_ _unless they take the time now to prepare. The last time anything close to this happened in America, our economy suffered a large surprise crash. The market netted 0% over the following sixteen years. And by the end, adjusted for inflation, American investors lost 80% of their wealth. This time, I expect much worse."_
To defend yourself - and potentially profit...
He recommends you consider [moving money to a new type of investment, right away](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/fL7ejAiV_WRcOc4QwuzcOQ~~/AARclAA~/RgRpTCFCP0SSaHR0cHM6Ly90cmFja2luZy5jbGlja3N0cmlwZTEuY29tL2FmZl9jP29mZmVyX2lkPTIxMzkmYWZmX2lkPTEwOTQmc291cmNlPUVtYWlsJnN1YjE9e3RyYW5zYWN0aW9uX2lkfSZzdWIyPXBvcDEyMDEyNGEmc3ViMz02NDgmc3ViND1STS1VQ04tMjAyNDEyMjNXA3NwY0IKZ2VCjWlnsAt91FIUZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb21YBAAAAAA~). (It's not gold, real estate, or cryptocurrency.)
Because time is running low, Ferris has agreed to [publish his latest findings online, for free](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/fL7ejAiV_WRcOc4QwuzcOQ~~/AARclAA~/RgRpTCFCP0SSaHR0cHM6Ly90cmFja2luZy5jbGlja3N0cmlwZTEuY29tL2FmZl9jP29mZmVyX2lkPTIxMzkmYWZmX2lkPTEwOTQmc291cmNlPUVtYWlsJnN1YjE9e3RyYW5zYWN0aW9uX2lkfSZzdWIyPXBvcDEyMDEyNGEmc3ViMz02NDgmc3ViND1STS1VQ04tMjAyNDEyMjNXA3NwY0IKZ2VCjWlnsAt91FIUZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb21YBAAAAAA~).
[Click here to see his new message now](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/fL7ejAiV_WRcOc4QwuzcOQ~~/AARclAA~/RgRpTCFCP0SSaHR0cHM6Ly90cmFja2luZy5jbGlja3N0cmlwZTEuY29tL2FmZl9jP29mZmVyX2lkPTIxMzkmYWZmX2lkPTEwOTQmc291cmNlPUVtYWlsJnN1YjE9e3RyYW5zYWN0aW9uX2lkfSZzdWIyPXBvcDEyMDEyNGEmc3ViMz02NDgmc3ViND1STS1VQ04tMjAyNDEyMjNXA3NwY0IKZ2VCjWlnsAt91FIUZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb21YBAAAAAA~).
Regards,
Brett Aitken
Publisher, Stansberry Research

US Conservative Network

_PO Box 1177 Fort Collins, CO 80522_

# FROM THE DESK OF LARRY KUDLOW
---
| | Reader,**I am pleased to share with you that The New York Sun, one of my daily must-read papers, is now the exclusive publisher of my regular columns.

I invite you to consider joining the Sun to receive unlimited access to my latest commentary, alongside some of the finest American journalism.

**But there's also far more to what the Sun is building.

As you know, journalism isn't what it used to be.

I've been in the business for decades, and never before have I seen this once noble profession so abused. The major papers are no longer on the side of the American people, and we are right to withhold our trust.

The impact on our democracy is considerable. Without real accountability, the worst actors in government and at corporate institutions have free reign, and patriotic, hard-working Americans are being sidelined and ignored.

The antidote is to join the movement to build a better alternative. That's the American way.

**The charge to restore American journalism is being led by the Sun.** A proudly all-American, principled publication that has plenty of room for conservative thought and conservative writers, the Sun has upheld the highest journalistic standards since 1833. That's 18 years before the New York Times published its first number.**My decision for the New York Sun to become the exclusive publisher of my regular columns was not taken lightly.**

I value their work and have been impressed by their team, but I know they have a hell of a battle ahead. They are the David facing many Goliaths.

They will only succeed if we join with them in this grassroots national effort. Joining the Sun costs as little as a penny a day. I encourage you to sign up today. Every single person counts.
---

**Remember:** If they take the House, they can stonewall EVERY America First Agenda item President Trump tries to pass through Congress.

**Remember:** If they jam up Trump's agenda, everything we fought so hard for this year will completely COLLAPSE.

name: Gabby Giffords
subject: Thank you so much in advance
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Giffords PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
I hope when you think of the organizations you want to support this holiday season, you think of GIFFORDS and our work to save lives from gun violence.
#

[](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-dec24.MAKoxe&refcode=em241223-1-b&refcode2=9684.3935704.5uNMUO&t=1&akid=9684%2E3935704%2E5uNMUO)
---
Hi friends, it’s Gabby.
The team and I will be taking a break to spend time with loved ones over the next few days. It’s been a busy year, so I hope you take some well-deserved time to rest too.
However, even a short break means our fundraising will slow down drastically over the next couple of days. With our end of year FEC fundraising deadline approaching rapidly, we need to do everything we can to set ourselves up for success to reach our goals for the year ahead.
[**So I’m asking you respectfully: Before we head offline for the holidays, will you make a $3 contribution to GIFFORDS PAC?**](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-dec24.MAKoxe&refcode=em241223-1-b&t=2&akid=9684%2E3935704%2E5uNMUO)
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly:_
| | **[Donate $3](https://go.giffords.org/go/17015?c0=giffords-em-dec24.YP__Dn&amount=3&refcode=em241223-1-b&t=21&akid=9684%2E3935704%2E5uNMUO)**
---
****
| **[Donate $25](https://go.giffords.org/go/17017?c0=giffords-em-dec24.Tl-4ZJ&refcode=em241223-1-b&t=22&akid=9684%2E3935704%2E5uNMUO)**
---
****
| | **[Donate $50](https://go.giffords.org/go/17010?c0=giffords-em-dec24.Wil-xB&refcode=em241223-1-b&t=23&akid=9684%2E3935704%2E5uNMUO)**
---
****
| **[Donate $100](https://go.giffords.org/go/17011?c0=giffords-em-dec24.5v9WVG&refcode=em241223-1-b&t=24&akid=9684%2E3935704%2E5uNMUO)**
---
****
| | **[Donate $250](https://go.giffords.org/go/17012?c0=giffords-em-dec24.DV10SI&refcode=em241223-1-b&t=25&akid=9684%2E3935704%2E5uNMUO)**
---
****
| **[Other Amount](https://go.giffords.org/go/17015?c0=giffords-em-dec24.YP__Dn&refcode=em241223-1-b&t=26&akid=9684%2E3935704%2E5uNMUO)**
---
****
I know that you’re most likely receiving asks from many different organizations and campaigns, especially at this time of year. With so many worthy causes out there, I understand that you’re not able to give to everyone.
But I hope when you think of the organizations you want to support this holiday season, you think of GIFFORDS and our work to save lives from gun violence. We couldn’t do any of our work to make our communities safer without you.
[**So please, if you can, make a contribution to GIFFORDS PAC today.**](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-dec24.MAKoxe&refcode=em241223-1-b&t=27&akid=9684%2E3935704%2E5uNMUO)
Thank you in advance for chipping in. Your support—especially today—is instrumental in our ability to reach our 2024 goals.
Happy holidays!
All my best,
Gabby Giffords
**[CONTRIBUTE](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-dec24.MAKoxe&refcode=em241223-1-b&t=28&akid=9684%2E3935704%2E5uNMUO)**

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. We try to send only the most important information and opportunities to participate via email, but click [here](https://go.giffords.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=29&akid=9684%2E3935704%2E5uNMUO) if you'd like to unsubscribe. Giffords PAC was started to support elected officials who step up to fight the gun violence epidemic. Thank you for standing with Gabby and the Giffords team.
clean_body: I hope when you think of the organizations you want to support this holiday season, you think of GIFFORDS and our work to save lives from gun violence. Hi friends, it’s Gabby. The team and I will be taking a break to spend time with loved ones over the next few days. It’s been a busy year, so I hope you take some well-deserved time to rest too. However, even a short break means our fundraising will slow down drastically over the next couple of days. With our end of year FEC fundraising deadline approaching rapidly, we need to do everything we can to set ourselves up for success to reach our goals for the year ahead. If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly: | | ** | | | | | | | I know that you’re most likely receiving asks from many different organizations and campaigns, especially at this time of year. With so many worthy causes out there, I understand that you’re not able to give to everyone. But I hope when you think of the organizations you want to support this holiday season, you think of GIFFORDS and our work to save lives from gun violence. We couldn’t do any of our work to make our communities safer without you. Thank you in advance for chipping in. Your support—especially today—is instrumental in our ability to reach our 2024 goals. Happy holidays! All my best, Gabby Giffords ** Paid For By Giffords PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. www.Giffords.org

Peter – Pennsylvania elections are rotten to their core... and we won PA anyway.****
**But my fight isn’t over.**

[](http://links.conservativeum.com/u/click?_t=26c2e4aa9bbe40418f61e26972138f21&_m=be681476d16943518b1842f9ad33255a&_e=ESkOWNrrhIw3vPjy_jNiHAVjlOA3H07twXZWX-1aD-cvXzpd6TSzhKOSyRLE0J91oRUy3GknHf_yangjQYQcOlpyGPkyngOD9of6sOyxgmcnroSxUrwe6DhDp1OAre6J93omcjWiowqCOQdThrFOSCRv454uGW_YY6gFXnyd3EgiYwgg_eCUizFG6pFe0Q2Fw5MRRI1LAH_y7jNcPXye5-ff82tEX_34bmOdb-u_4WcLMfIpuhoVhVOkw3eMUwnGg6BOsP8Sd2vafoOZYesVh3sFNE497AzBt0vEAO-rs9pPxxRi29wPFJWgraZ0kizvqPeJnC4gCZSN_i_UOgWJOt2J9IJX2IhAah09IBuUAZC-3BaIKWm5l3VvkhSb5cgK)
The actions of corrupt Democrat officials like **Bucks County Commissioner Diane Marseglia** and **left-wing activists like Marc Elias** prove that our work is just beginning.****
**I’ll never stop until we fully secure the integrity of our elections in Pennsylvania and every state in the union.**

With your help, I proved that Early Vote Action was an unstoppable grassroots force, and I’m gonna prove it again.

We’ve got an unprecedented opportunity to drive out corruption where it lives.

Just recently, I told corrupt Diane Marseglia that we’re going to take her seat... **_and they haven’t stopped coming after me since._**

The election is over, but the real work begins now... **_I’m not backing down, but I need_**** __your help__****.****____**
**__[Will you help me continue to fight for election integrity and take our country back](http://links.conservativeum.com/u/click?_t=26c2e4aa9bbe40418f61e26972138f21&_m=be681476d16943518b1842f9ad33255a&_e=ESkOWNrrhIw3vPjy_jNiHAVjlOA3H07twXZWX-1aD-cvXzpd6TSzhKOSyRLE0J91oRUy3GknHf_yangjQYQcOlpyGPkyngOD9of6sOyxgmcnroSxUrwe6DhDp1OAre6J93omcjWiowqCOQdThrFOSCRv454uGW_YY6gFXnyd3EgiYwgg_eCUizFG6pFe0Q2Fw5MRRI1LAH_y7jNcPXye53Ajl7zB0WT0xut4fnyIF6UK6ZGHgejRV7Guv69w2d876ISNp-Y75yUgiyFWsjVjGSkVSu-Rftcqe3c1JRFRkjcZ1gA8ASBq8ifv6GDmGtzcv4DaEL2xVlL97gy3KG4cwqKkJWy4ZabgOZAnoafoifiyzbxt6uEu5fZ8MeYccucE)_?_**

_God bless you,_
Scott Presler

name: Working Families Party
subject: We made a list (and checked it twice)
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Working Families Party PAC (77 Sands St
body:
Everyone on this list put their own greed for money or power over the wellbeing of our communities. | | | Peter, The holiday season is a time for reflection, a time to ask ourselves who among us has been nice this year — **_and who is getting coal?_** The Working Families Party has been making a list (_and_ checking it twice) of some of 2024’s top coal-earners, and we’re excited to share it with you today. **[But before we do: can you make an end-of-year donation to WFP so we can continue to stand up to our nation’s most misbehaved in 2025? Every contribution — no matter the amount — helps make sure we have the resources to fight back next year.](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfquqxZ_WMtWQ5nsYnjR236T9IMCZqG23MZNa_R3nK0XZCN1Llm_iq605UX_h9nau6NLjYieLkpb8yA4mf_Oyk6oRu2fC8BzvwEEWqu8LT6_USx4b8seaXprncjiZz7Ag1wtg8TyeX9VzEOO62KxEHwAuDMbiv0NRyk5zfGu8e4_RwY_XLX_zb1dM9WOdEArJC6YQv7z0gicAVOm7vGpIiWaoj7diM3cytYe8L81qmFb-odaxx0kAI1sh0bXHJjIQablnw3OjixUeKZe5g6ZdLztRd7bopy4U1IOJ3-ASz7Mi5NQ5JVCUYvCCrzJxD2PDYKbQWgaqQOsgdJU6gAg_E57YMqYPSURDY9HyISvOyl9MaA/4cj/SYLEqu2zRna61X_IdooS5Q/h0/h001.oT62oMC5bLYq-kP1AgOKa1QnG1CUphixU20DV-hwhEU)** **Presenting: The WFP 2024 Naughty List**
_(in no particular order)_ [](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfquqxZ_WMtWQ5nsYnjR236T9IMCZqG23MZNa_R3nK0XZCN1Llm_iq605UX_h9nau6NLjYieLkpb8yA4mf_Oyk6oRwuGnN2vYoaonecS5cTdlwZoRm1uL_Eijq51vFuhP1yyTYobjFNTshF6ImL-qM4tYatLwTMwrqoRHsUuxM3LhTnCL2WEtlCixmJvP8J1RwboPyRxxB9atkm15lDpEUXnZcPUgJb4sthh2W6myh8968aDZekhXrBO1zE8gyD0wbd8r0drC7UNMQN4MwLM7JvY4W2RDC0FCDXb3FEuugXaNkioBuuEueDXpN7K_4r05CSeaLjRaM95nuNWRrea9kw-2qKuBzqDJsxKSOg-eZiZAQg/4cj/SYLEqu2zRna61X_IdooS5Q/h1/h001.L551YYf31SW-KEkAmVWjhxF7XcJmd3yK1kGHpWh0ht0) **Donald Trump and JD Vance.** These men are deserving of a truly terrible holiday season, full of coal. They’ve spent their year lying to the American public about immigrants, about trans kids, about people who require abortion care, about their extremist Project 2025 plan to remake the federal government to their liking. They’ve spread fear and hatred everywhere they’ve gone, and it doesn’t get much less nice than that. **Elon Musk.** After buying Twitter, Musk set his sights higher: the United States of America. He poured a disgusting amount of money into electing Trump and other MAGA Republicans ($277 _million,_ making him the largest political donor of 2024). Now, he’s reaping the benefits of his investment by trying to take _our_ benefits away. Musk is setting his sights on cuts to Social Security, Medicare, and Medicaid for hardworking Americans who have been paying into these programs for years, and who knows what essential services he’ll try to cut next. Coal. **The far-right majority on the Supreme Court** — particularly its two most corrupt members, Clarence Thomas and Samuel Alito. These justices have made catastrophic decisions this year, from saying that a president cannot be held criminally accountable for their actions in office to taking away the federal government’s ability to determine standards for clean water, clean air, and more. Throughout 2024, this extremist Supreme Court majority proved that the only people they care about are the ultra-wealthy folks buying them off, and the convicted felon Donald Trump who stole some of their seats for them. That’s six servings of coal, coming right up. **Price-gouging billionaires** who line their own pockets at the expense of the rest of us. Essentials like groceries, housing, healthcare, education, and more have gotten harder and harder for us and our loved ones to afford. The reason for that is _corporate greed._ Billionaire CEOs and other executives are taking massive holiday bonuses this year while refusing to pay their employees their fair share and jacking up prices for their customers. They’ve chosen to hurt families in order to buy themselves yet another vacation home. We’d give them coal, but we’ve all already given them enough. **Super PACs and special interest groups** buying our elections — especially the Republican megadonors funding AIPAC, who targeted WFP champions like Jamaal Bowman and Cori Bush for standing up for working people and speaking out against the Israeli government’s war crimes in Gaza. The wealthiest in our country continue to have an outsized influence on our elections, drowning out the voices of the actual voters who our elected officials are supposed to represent. Coal all the way around. **[Everyone on this list put their own greed for money or power over the wellbeing of our communities. We aren't about to sit back and let them keep hurting the people we care about in 2025. Pitch in with an end-of-year donation to help the Working Families Party stand up to them and build power for the multiracial working class instead.](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfquqxZ_WMtWQ5nsYnjR236T9IMCZqG23MZNa_R3nK0XZCN1Llm_iq605UX_h9nau6NLjYieLkpb8yA4mf_Oyk6oRyOr7n3jvILA4cXZe90meKJRBg6Ja5h7hzzkQDqn96R1fcB6cXpGD9F35JyHyNA6ITCx9CWvVkbSZa24RRu5WJl2nrpimZTP5-o6UH6UyYwebMpQ8bWKi1Biglpw4oM0B_fWVZ_7WzeX3NSg7npLN8F1QmPyOCxqCh21YVdAPtmpFiBxK9NsAXmiZeI58PM594k0ysmIv-gYs6uXEr8XPIJUIxPijKnmKngOS9z_nz1q_vSr0_yqbb5Rj6XM3W-7bdxCl5Xbkq1g1elMV3SpJ5A/4cj/SYLEqu2zRna61X_IdooS5Q/h2/h001.ivJPmZwIdA3rXWovVoqY6Tq_VjSRzqmI3kc9eMjmV0U)** clean_body: Everyone on this list put their own greed for money or power over the wellbeing of our communities. | | | Peter, The holiday season is a time for reflection, a time to ask ourselves who among us has been nice this year — and who is getting coal? The Working Families Party has been making a list (and checking it twice) of some of 2024’s top coal-earners, and we’re excited to share it with you today. Presenting: The WFP 2024 Naughty List (in no particular order) Donald Trump and JD Vance. These men are deserving of a truly terrible holiday season, full of coal. They’ve spent their year lying to the American public about immigrants, about trans kids, about people who require abortion care, about their extremist Project 2025 plan to remake the federal government to their liking. They’ve spread fear and hatred everywhere they’ve gone, and it doesn’t get much less nice than that. Elon Musk. After buying Twitter, Musk set his sights higher: the United States of America. He poured a disgusting amount of money into electing Trump and other MAGA Republicans ($277 million, making him the largest political donor of 2024). Now, he’s reaping the benefits of his investment by trying to take our benefits away. Musk is setting his sights on cuts to Social Security, Medicare, and Medicaid for hardworking Americans who have been paying into these programs for years, and who knows what essential services he’ll try to cut next. Coal. The far-right majority on the Supreme Court — particularly its two most corrupt members, Clarence Thomas and Samuel Alito. These justices have made catastrophic decisions this year, from saying that a president cannot be held criminally accountable for their actions in office to taking away the federal government’s ability to determine standards for clean water, clean air, and more. Throughout 2024, this extremist Supreme Court majority proved that the only people they care about are the ultra-wealthy folks buying them off, and the convicted felon Donald Trump who stole some of their seats for them. That’s six servings of coal, coming right up. Price-gouging billionaires who line their own pockets at the expense of the rest of us. Essentials like groceries, housing, healthcare, education, and more have gotten harder and harder for us and our loved ones to afford. The reason for that is corporate greed. Billionaire CEOs and other executives are taking massive holiday bonuses this year while refusing to pay their employees their fair share and jacking up prices for their customers. They’ve chosen to hurt families in order to buy themselves yet another vacation home. We’d give them coal, but we’ve all already given them enough. Super PACs and special interest groups buying our elections — especially the Republican megadonors funding AIPAC, who targeted WFP champions like Jamaal Bowman and Cori Bush for standing up for working people and speaking out against the Israeli government’s war crimes in Gaza. The wealthiest in our country continue to have an outsized influence on our elections, drowning out the voices of the actual voters who our elected officials are supposed to represent. Coal all the way around. In solidarity, Working Families Party Thoughts? Feedback? We want to hear it. Reply directly to this email and let us know. This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you received this email as a forward and want to get the latest updates from the Working Families Party directly, The Working Families Party is the party of the multiracial working class, fighting for a nation that cares for all of us. Wanna learn more? And if you need to update your contact information, Other political groups have corporate donors. But we can pool our resources too. Paid for by Working Families Party PAC (77 Sands St. #6, Brooklyn, NY, 11201). | | Sent via . To update your email address, change your name or address, or to stop receiving emails from Working Families, please .

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/99168040/519063367/-1080040549?refcode=email-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzg4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzMmI5MmY1OS00ZWMxLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDIyNDgyYTkzYWYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=PtEW4K_UgEbbdHN3wDDpZn5iEZGBMd-n6drlQXRbFyw=) Happy Holidays! Did you know Oregon is the top producer of Christmas trees in the country? This time of year, I’m particularly grateful for the hardworking farmers in our community who help bring so much holiday cheer into our homes (like this beautiful tree my husband picked out). In the past, my daughter Amelia would eagerly write her wish list to Santa, and I’d find myself thinking about my own wishes, too. But this year, I feel like I already have everything I could hope for. Because of your support, I get to wake up every day and do what I love: standing up for our community and making a difference. I hope your holiday season is filled with warmth and joy. Happy Holidays! \- Andrea

I hope as the year comes to a close, you’re surrounded by your loved ones and able to reflect back on an incredible year to celebrate all you’ve accomplished.

Serving you and your community in New Jersey’s 12th District is an honor I am thankful for every day. I am so grateful for all the support I receive from folks like yourself, and I am humbled to have earned your trust once again this election season.

I want to take this opportunity to recommit myself to working hard to fight for our community in Congress in 2025. Together, there’s nothing we can’t achieve this coming year.

Wishing you a warm, joyful holiday season. Thank you once again for your support.

— Bonnie

**Trump won because his campaign of fear and lies worked. **

The hard truth is that the right’s media dominance is working. And until we build a progressive media network strong enough to rival their disinformation machine, their lies will keep winning and our democracy will be in peril.

name: Ohio Democrats
subject: Loser LaRose might fill Vance’s Senate seat… we have thoughts.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Ohio Democratic Party and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
Let’s take a trip down memory lane
|
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/99168274/519064161/-2045038890?refcode=ODP_ATA_EM_FR_LaRose_241223_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84OTI0NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MzBlNmIwZi00ZmMxLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDIyNDgyYTkzYWYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CuQ7szTgq4U9MR3oXIxIP3OS_qY9jOy5OP2UkfkPGBI=) Team,

Have you seen the news that one of the frontrunners to fill J.D. Vance’s Senate seat is none other than Loser Frank LaRose? Shocker, right? One power hungry politician gives up his current job to get a new one and then fills that position with the _next_ power hungry politician in line.

In a moment, we’re going to **[ask you to chip in $5](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/99168275/519064162/-2045038890?amount=5&refcode=ODP_ATA_EM_FR_LaRose_241223_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84OTI0NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MzBlNmIwZi00ZmMxLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDIyNDgyYTkzYWYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CuQ7szTgq4U9MR3oXIxIP3OS_qY9jOy5OP2UkfkPGBI=)** to help us stop LaRose from gaining even more power, but first, we want to stroll down Loser LaRose memory lane:

In 2024 alone, here’s what LaRose was up to:
* He purged hundreds of thousands of Ohio voters from the voter rolls ahead of the election

* He drafted deceptive and manipulative ballot language to deceive voters on the anti-gerrymandering amendment

* He put forward a directive that banned the use of drop boxes by individuals returning a ballot on behalf of an immediate family member or a disabled voter

* And he ran for Senate in the Ohio GOP primary... and lost

LaRose’s job is to help Ohioans vote, but he tries to make it harder every chance he gets – because all he cares about is status, power, and himself.

[_**While we don’t know exactly what’s ahead for Loser LaRose, we do know that we will stand up against his anti-democratic tricks every step of the way, like we always have. Will you pitch in to join us in this fight?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/99168276/519064163/-2045038890?refcode=ODP_ATA_EM_FR_LaRose_241223_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84OTI0NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MzBlNmIwZi00ZmMxLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDIyNDgyYTkzYWYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CuQ7szTgq4U9MR3oXIxIP3OS_qY9jOy5OP2UkfkPGBI=)

Thank you,

Ohio Democrats

name: Seth Moulton
subject: The clock is ticking for Democrats
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Seth Moulton for Congress
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/seth-moulton-3 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/seth-moulton-3]
Trump is back. With every new loyalist cabinet pick, that reality sets in. The Republican trifecta will drive the agenda.

And now the clock is ticking for Democrats. Do we go back to the same old playbook and keep losing, or do we start changing now so that we can win?

We’ve lost support everywhere. One of the biggest reasons is that Democrats can’t seem to have honest conversations with Americans about what matters in their lives. We’re more concerned about strict ideological positions than fixing problems. We can’t even tolerate enough reasonable debate on social issues to counter MAGA’s cruelest tactics.

That’s why I recently said something that I know a lot of people agree with: Democrats seem to care more about not offending anyone than actually winning. I wrote about it recently in a Washington Post op-ed. [https://wapo.st/3Ow6XHH]

But now I’m likely facing a primary challenge simply for saying what so many have been thinking.

I’ve never been one to shy away from a fight. I’m a proud Democrat, and I’m pushing our party because I’m sick of ceding ground to MAGA extremists. You should be, too.

We need to win, and if you agree, I’ll need your help starting today. Please consider making an early investment in my re-election campaign here. [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/seth-moulton-3]

Thanks for all you do.

-Seth

Donate [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/seth-moulton-3]

name: House GOP
subject: The Lefts plan to sabotage Trumps House Majority
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRCC and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
This could bet ugly

|
---
| | |
Peter,

**The Left has found its first opportunity to cause chaos during Donald Trump 's Presidency: buying the FL-1 and FL-6 House seats in the upcoming Special Elections. **

Democrats are attempting to flip these open seats and infiltrate **RED** Florida with **BLUE** liberals.

Peter, it's safe to say that our America First legislative agenda will be in grave danger if Nancy Pelosi and her liberal billionaires take these seats.

And they're not going down without a fight: the Left will spend BIG to take these seats because they know how important they are!

Peter, we can't afford to let the Left take this opportunity to slow down Trump's agenda. If we have any shot at winning this thing, we need to catch up to the Left's endless cash reserve.

name: Blackburn, Marsha
subject: I have two things for you today, Peter
body: ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ Y’all – this is Marsha Blackburn. I have two things for you today. I’ll make it quick, I promise. 1) I know you get a lot of email updates from my team and me so as we approach the new year, I just wanted to take a moment to express just how grateful I am to have had you by my side in this past election. I am truly so humbled and honored for all of the support and encouragement you’ve shown me over the course of this campaign. Month after month you showed your commitment - your prayers, signatures, and donations made all the difference in the end. None of this would’ve been possible without you. 2) My team recently re-ordered our best-selling Team Marsha T-Shirts, and we have a few left in stock. There’s only a limited quantity available, and we likely won’t re-order now that the election cycle is over, but the last time these were in stock they flew off the shelves! So, as a small token of my appreciation for all of your support, I have put one on hold for you in our Merch Store and attached your confirmation below: MERCH STORE HOLD CONFIRMATION Hold Status: Confirmed ID Code: https://click.updates.marshablackburn.com/?qs=59ccee28832e09ef9a28cbe635d0c02e6ac285f54d9f898d0edf7ae7bf29db655e010b6ecc747a01bd68df54aa0c725968f790ab1bb7a06541c9f9d3486364eb dpwillis67@gmail.com-23-005 Item: “Team Marsha” T-Shirt Expiration: 12/26/24 @ 11:59 PM (ET) https://click.updates.marshablackburn.com/?qs=59ccee28832e09ef9a28cbe635d0c02e6ac285f54d9f898d0edf7ae7bf29db655e010b6ecc747a01bd68df54aa0c725968f790ab1bb7a06541c9f9d3486364eb https://click.updates.marshablackburn.com/?qs=59ccee28832e09ef9a28cbe635d0c02e6ac285f54d9f898d0edf7ae7bf29db655e010b6ecc747a01bd68df54aa0c725968f790ab1bb7a06541c9f9d3486364eb >" alias="CLAIM YOUR T-SHIRT HERE >>" conversion="false" data-linkto="https://">CLAIM YOUR T-SHIRT HERE >> I don’t expect you to read your email every second of every day, especially at such a busy time of year, so I’m giving you a window to see this and claim your item. Your hold reservation expires at midnight on December 26th. Only the most dedicated members of my team are being offered the chance to claim an official “Team Marsha” t-shirt from our remaining limited stock, https://click.updates.marshablackburn.com/?qs=59ccee28832e09ef9a28cbe635d0c02e6ac285f54d9f898d0edf7ae7bf29db655e010b6ecc747a01bd68df54aa0c725968f790ab1bb7a06541c9f9d3486364eb so I really hope you take this opportunity today. Thank you again! I won’t ever forget your dedication to my fight, and your support will always mean the world. - Marsha Blackburn Paid for by Blackburn Tennessee Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Marsha for Senate, MARSHA PAC, and Tennessee Republican Party Federal Election Account. name: EMILYs List
subject: Wishing you a joyful holiday season! 🎁
body:

name: Shawnna Bolick
subject: December Update
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY VOTE BOLICK
body:
| | | [ ](https://bolickforarizona.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3214fdbd528e32d06cf84fc86&id=d0f584e7fb&e=a7ab36849c)
---
| | Dear Freedom Fighters,

Good morning from San Cristobal Island via Starlink access.

It surely has been a jam-packed month leading up to Christmas. As many of you may know, the real work begins after the election has been won. I have been busy doing research and meeting with constituents who are interested in solving problems facing our state. I had started to compile a short list of suggestions earlier this year after the introduction of bills deadline in the Senate had passed. Of course, it grows over the many months while we are not physically at the Capitol from individuals I meet along the campaign trail, or individuals who contact this campaign email and of course, my legislative office looking for assistance. Thankfully, many issues brought to me don’t need a legislative fix, but often a call or a nudge to a government bureaucrat to nudge the paperwork sitting on someone’s desk that might need to be approved. I have opened a few bill folders for legislation to be drafted, but I haven’t filed any bills, yet.

I attended the Honest Election Project Academy earlier in the month. Beginning late last year I worked with the staff at Honest Election Project and into this year to write a bill to ban foreign donations in our state’s election administration. I will be introducing this bill once again because it is a commonsense measure that was stopped by the soon-to-be former Chair of the House Committee on Elections. The name of the organization might sound familiar to you. Honest Election Project Action (HEPA) helped educate Arizona voters throughout this election cycle on the pitfalls of adopting ranked choice voting. HEPA continued to illustrate how the opposition campaign was lying to voters because ranked choice voting is not fair in any way. Thankfully, the Arizona electorate [voted down Proposition 140](https://bolickforarizona.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3214fdbd528e32d06cf84fc86&id=e87225ea19&e=a7ab36849c) last month.

Earlier this month I hosted a legislative cyber security briefing for the members of both legislative chambers. It is worth noting District 2 is home to the [Arizona Counter Terrorism Information Center](https://bolickforarizona.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3214fdbd528e32d06cf84fc86&id=8469d352d9&e=a7ab36849c) so it’s always good to be on top of what past and current cyber security type attacks might impact our state’s well-being. I had a chance to get a briefing from a local organization after the CrowdStrike cyber-attack earlier this summer, so it was good to get an update and be able to share this same information with my fellow members of both political parties so we have the same access to resources available.

As usual I have been balancing family events with legislative duties. Earlier this month we traveled to California to attend our future daughter-in-law’s bridal shower and to get to know her extended family and friends a little better before their wedding in January. I also wrote this December update to you from Ecuador. We flew down here for a handful of days to snorkel with hammerheads and sea lions and to see the giant tortoise up close. Thankfully, I believe we saw everything endemic to both islands we visited on this short but jam-packed trip. We make our way back to Arizona this morning, but won’t arrive in Phoenix until late on Christmas Eve since you need to dedicate two days of traveling in each direction to reach your destination.

If you are in the Valley and you’re looking for something fun to do with friends and family who might be visiting the Valley for the holidays, I highly suggest taking in Desert Botanical Gardens’ Luminarias display. Our family heads out each year. This year we went right after Thanksgiving knowing how busy the month of December would be for us.

Merry Christmas, Happy Hanukkah and a very Happy New Year to you and yours!

Best,
Shawnna

---

name: Bill Gray
subject: Experience the Joy of Dickens’ A Christmas Carol
body:
_**A message from our sponsor...**_

| | | [](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=8034d3937d1d48e49b82812ae072b577&_e=tV5LO_EK0jaSYjq_1aZcUufEPb0eb8jCnX0GqoBFes8wB8MtPM_mw6zUcEBwT_QfYz2JNkLByLkp8RKMuXhTqztpD6wWqLtzUptQfWAgE4hwX3A7_fwF9YUX3jBiBPqvciT1FfNPIrIDkPVHMNzPHiEw4ioREt2rHUjabnKy85ttlvlfdeCIsoaxcRddFPx6QzQkrCNh3CtpmoB0LJEWd1KAsF8e0l5rq_RDp53j1qp-bFbqkB-_xSoVQXjZX2clIdBi2r-97iqG4MnePheMpz56VHAg8HenjPcmf71C5iUQt55DsLfmtBurR7lKFrRbsjJrujITq8EXuaSB0jw4ikRfuLtEFEML5z4bJkGyGYnW81H6_AMrpjxjWhh8Dr1bRwbzhAp1Qdgv0O98Z5lKKsqxDg2jhZ6sWu0VL6FeMcI%3D)

| | Peter,

Great literature teaches you about human nature and draws you toward the good. Charles Dickens' _A Christmas Carol_ is a wonderful example.

You're likely familiar with Ebenezer Scrooge--literature's most famous miser. His choices corrupted his soul and left him miserable. The drama of this beloved Christmas tale consists of his education at the hands of the three Ghosts of Christmas and its effects on his soul.

The story is rich with meaning. Among its chief lessons is that one's happiness depends upon learning to choose well and to love the good. [](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=8034d3937d1d48e49b82812ae072b577&_e=tV5LO_EK0jaSYjq_1aZcUufEPb0eb8jCnX0GqoBFes8wB8MtPM_mw6zUcEBwT_QfYz2JNkLByLkp8RKMuXhTqztpD6wWqLtzUptQfWAgE4hwX3A7_fwF9YUX3jBiBPqvciT1FfNPIrIDkPVHMNzPHiEw4ioREt2rHUjabnKy85ttlvlfdeCIsoaxcRddFPx6QzQkrCNh3CtpmoB0LJEWd1KAsF8e0l5rq_RDp53j1qp-bFbqkB-_xSoVQXjZX2clO1l_IYWHdVvw5sBxS0_cjFVGMC64p1O11e5ruiDQIu4LbyvkbXAU6kCqX1SeekbNfvGzGMDK8HwfihBTsKZ4U7zGQ8XPaAdWj1_r_2JdJpV6COt5LnK50utbz_ywHgh0Mnz7RG333B6EcDWULuAWPHgkP-rXMD7q4y9cHHRvdPc%3D)
**This Christmas, I want to send you an exclusive hardcover Hillsdale College edition of _A Christmas Carol_ for your tax-deductible gift of $100 or more.**

This beautiful volume features an inspiring introduction and 26 exclusive illustrations. You can treasure this meaningful keepsake or even gift it to a loved one.

[https://secured.hillsdale.edu/hillsdale/christmas-carol-book](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=8034d3937d1d48e49b82812ae072b577&_e=tV5LO_EK0jaSYjq_1aZcUufEPb0eb8jCnX0GqoBFes8wB8MtPM_mw6zUcEBwT_QfYz2JNkLByLkp8RKMuXhTqztpD6wWqLtzUptQfWAgE4hwX3A7_fwF9YUX3jBiBPqvciT1FfNPIrIDkPVHMNzPHiEw4ioREt2rHUjabnKy85ttlvlfdeCIsoaxcRddFPx6QzQkrCNh3CtpmoB0LJEWd1KAsF8e0l5rq_RDp53j1qp-bFbqkB-_xSoVQXjZX2clUaY7Qosas3n18P-d7HBJd49siFoQGb8PYj8YryOskRIaaWJZIJImCS7xLo75TQDrqUGzqHSi1b6ttqgldUNEDmtQszrsfIB_TkEPNrXi1N7irIAR2mG4th9VdfZ5y4OFxQFh0cWFbHK2z1XTsQtchwEpARLj-6_9q4-LfzSrrKQ%3D)

I wish you and yours a blessed Christmas season,

Bill Gray, '01
Vice President
Institutional Advancement
Hillsdale College [

* **TOP NEWS**

“**EXCLUSIVE: Where is Congresswoman Kay Granger?** ” Dallas Express' **Carlos Turcios** — “Amid the ongoing drama in D.C. with Congress furiously debating and voting on an interim spending bill a key question has arisen from constituents in Texas Congressional District 12, “Where is Congresswoman **Kay Granger**?”

According to Ms. Granger’s roll call [vote](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.DYoB0HdcAL_Q6gFRSa_l6ZhftaB961qFKNBmyUPB1bL-fQvHHhqqNWHRhDruJIDPHsqK6Fy3IcrKJgyvxj294VW7scvxKNqBxHgZuPcXmwqVlQlkNWkyWrASxiHs0AJURSz_-NNqeFzSWNQv71gQw9QkXLvsvQU2YbfDio1z1nJqMl1EoEeh4t5wyCbjfKaVsBr6ezM-aSPSYEK9wwDtxtu4BQcRbPUYnF-jsXHSSdFI6uwaeq-L5nhGQ_bWw38VUGVzq23Rj_s6bXV-g8GCFRpE0_KlQQ2rvenyY2MCrY4NAx7MWkDFHZTtCzPNlK6i-1i42SsjWsdm5OVBR_ryGwyZu7ZUaNOlHMPCN8TgQ9raGdueftQBFGcyn5ZSzcQfpa61hDkrwgdY17zwTOj2AMJ9S8XhekNxDwS5lFeYSftN5m-5cQOAoI7uIk1GdED--Z6HHMaCzElLPaUWBNqHv0TNddmPEjaOdfSAA2Yj1qg/4cj/D2MKUt4aTau-nCA3jI87fw/h15/h001.W2UTjxHzLiL21urczQfycLwr-Bpg0RY1oINFu_SJFQA) page, Granger's last vote was on July 24th, 2024 as she voted No to the “ _Amendment in the House_ H.Amdt. 1157 (Miller) to H.R. 8998: To reduce the salary of Ya-Wei (Jake) Li, Deputy Assistant Administrator for Pesticide Programs, to $1.”

Since then, Granger has not cast any votes in Washington on behalf of her Tarrant and Parker County constituents.

_The Dallas Express_ attempted to reach her district and D.C. offices but calls went directly to voicemail where a recorded message from Congresswoman Granger plays.

“I am sorry we are unable to answer your phone right now. We are really glad you called us. Please leave your name, phone number and a brief message and someone in our office will call you back as soon as possible,” the recorded message says.

We then visited her office in person hoping to understand how Congresswoman Granger planned to vote on the continuing resolution this afternoon. Upon arrival, we found the door locked, front door glass window covered, no one inside, and no sign of the office continuing to be occupied.

We then received a tip from a Granger constituent who shared that the Congresswoman has been residing at a local memory care and assisted living home for some time after having been found wandering lost and confused in her former Cultural District/West 7th neighborhood.

($5/mo or $50/yr or $199 lifetime) | TOP NEWS “EXCLUSIVE: Where is Congresswoman Kay Granger? ” Dallas Express' Carlos Turcios — “Amid the ongoing drama in D.C. with Congress furiously debating and voting on an interim spending bill a key question has arisen from constituents in Texas Congressional District 12, “Where is Congresswoman Kay Granger?” According to Ms. Granger’s roll call page, Granger's last vote was on July 24th, 2024 as she voted No to the “ Amendment in the House H.Amdt. 1157 (Miller) to H.R. 8998: To reduce the salary of Ya-Wei (Jake) Li, Deputy Assistant Administrator for Pesticide Programs, to $1.” Since then, Granger has not cast any votes in Washington on behalf of her Tarrant and Parker County constituents. The Dallas Express attempted to reach her district and D.C. offices but calls went directly to voicemail where a recorded message from Congresswoman Granger plays. “I am sorry we are unable to answer your phone right now. We are really glad you called us. Please leave your name, phone number and a brief message and someone in our office will call you back as soon as possible,” the recorded message says. We then visited her office in person hoping to understand how Congresswoman Granger planned to vote on the continuing resolution this afternoon. Upon arrival, we found the door locked, front door glass window covered, no one inside, and no sign of the office continuing to be occupied. When we asked employees at the WestBend building where Ms. Granger’s district office is located what happened to Ms. Granger’s office we were told that her team had packed up and closed the office before Thanksgiving. Her office appears to be closed for good (she is retiring at the end of the session) with phone calls going unanswered and voice mails not returned. We then received a tip from a Granger constituent who shared that the Congresswoman has been residing at a local memory care and assisted living home for some time after having been found wandering lost and confused in her former Cultural District/West 7th neighborhood. The Dallas Express team visited the facility to confirm whether Granger was residing there and to inquire about how she planned to vote on the spending bill. Upon arrival, two employees confirmed that Granger is indeed living at the facility. However, we were not permitted to conduct an interview regarding the current spending debate in the House of Representatives and how or if Ms. Granger planned to vote. Taylor Manziel who is the Assistant Executive Director for the senior living facility acknowledged to The Dallas Express that “This is her home.”" | FREE SUBSCRIPTIONS HAVE ENDED : As of July 11, 2022, we have moved to a paywall for free subscribers. body:
| | | [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/99172359/519078054/-1695911484?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImM5NmQ4Yzg0LTUzYzEtZWYxMS04OGNlLTAwMjI0ODJhOTNhZiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=qZjUqEyYFLpByyhNxWwp1tGRSqr8AEu_RZggBHc4Q5k=&emci=c521fff5-ffbe-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&emdi=c96d8c84-53c1-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&ceid=2107230)

# Dear Peter, I’m Anthony Broadman, Senator-elect for District 27, Central Oregon!
---
I have served on Bend’s City Council since 2020, so I’m thrilled to be able to represent Central Oregon in Salem. If you’re receiving this email, you are a part of OLCV’s vital work to elect pro-environment candidates, and I want to thank you for assisting my win in Senate District 27! To secure my win, OLCV knocked on nearly 3,000 doors, talking to voters about my campaign. That’s a _fourth_ of all the doors they knocked on in 2024! They also contributed $50,905 to my campaign, making them a _top three supporter_ _*_. None of this would have ever been possible if it weren’t for you. You’re a part of OLCV’s strong community, and I want to say thanks! [**As someone who prioritizes our climate and environment, I’m writing to ask you if you will continue to support OLCV in 2025 by making an end-of-year gift now.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/99172361/519078059/-1695911484?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImM5NmQ4Yzg0LTUzYzEtZWYxMS04OGNlLTAwMjI0ODJhOTNhZiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=qZjUqEyYFLpByyhNxWwp1tGRSqr8AEu_RZggBHc4Q5k=&emci=c521fff5-ffbe-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&emdi=c96d8c84-53c1-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&ceid=2107230) OLCV has been serving as Oregon’s political arm for the environment for over 50 years, securing wins for our climate year after year. OLCV gets the job done effectively, and the numbers prove that. I know OLCV will have my back this upcoming legislative session, as we fight for our climate together. **[Will you join us in making a donation to OLCV?](https://click.everyaction.com/k/99172362/519078062/-1695911484?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImM5NmQ4Yzg0LTUzYzEtZWYxMS04OGNlLTAwMjI0ODJhOTNhZiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=qZjUqEyYFLpByyhNxWwp1tGRSqr8AEu_RZggBHc4Q5k=&emci=c521fff5-ffbe-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&emdi=c96d8c84-53c1-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&ceid=2107230)**
---
[**DONATE NOW**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/99172363/519078063/-1695911484?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImM5NmQ4Yzg0LTUzYzEtZWYxMS04OGNlLTAwMjI0ODJhOTNhZiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=qZjUqEyYFLpByyhNxWwp1tGRSqr8AEu_RZggBHc4Q5k=&emci=c521fff5-ffbe-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&emdi=c96d8c84-53c1-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&ceid=2107230)
---
| | **Now is a great chance to make use of your free Political Tax Credit, if you haven’t already.**[**If you’re eligible, use your Political Tax Credit to make a free donation of $50 or $100 to the OLCV PAC here!**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/99172364/519078064/-1695911484?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImM5NmQ4Yzg0LTUzYzEtZWYxMS04OGNlLTAwMjI0ODJhOTNhZiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=qZjUqEyYFLpByyhNxWwp1tGRSqr8AEu_RZggBHc4Q5k=&emci=c521fff5-ffbe-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&emdi=c96d8c84-53c1-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&ceid=2107230)**** You are eligible if: ✔ You are an Oregon resident✔ You will submit an Oregon tax return for 2024 ✔ You make less than $75,000 (individual) or $150,000 (couple filing jointly)
For those who have already used your Political Tax Credit, [I hope you’ll consider making a year end contribution to OLCV to join us in this fight.](https://click.everyaction.com/k/99172365/519078065/-1698714710?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImM5NmQ4Yzg0LTUzYzEtZWYxMS04OGNlLTAwMjI0ODJhOTNhZiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=qZjUqEyYFLpByyhNxWwp1tGRSqr8AEu_RZggBHc4Q5k=&emci=c521fff5-ffbe-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&emdi=c96d8c84-53c1-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&ceid=2107230)
Thank you for your support and for being an OLCV member!
---
[**DONATE NOW**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/99172366/519078066/-1695911484?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImM5NmQ4Yzg0LTUzYzEtZWYxMS04OGNlLTAwMjI0ODJhOTNhZiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=qZjUqEyYFLpByyhNxWwp1tGRSqr8AEu_RZggBHc4Q5k=&emci=c521fff5-ffbe-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&emdi=c96d8c84-53c1-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&ceid=2107230)
---
Sincerely,
---

Anthony BroadmanSenator-Elect for Senate District 27 *OLCV was a top three supporter in my race excluding the Democratic caucus campaign and leadership. _P.S.: We are in the big push for end of year fundraising. If you would no longer like to receive emails related to end of year fundraising,_[_please fill out this form here to opt-out_](https://click.everyaction.com/k/99172367/519078067/357648456?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImM5NmQ4Yzg0LTUzYzEtZWYxMS04OGNlLTAwMjI0ODJhOTNhZiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=qZjUqEyYFLpByyhNxWwp1tGRSqr8AEu_RZggBHc4Q5k=&emci=c521fff5-ffbe-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&emdi=c96d8c84-53c1-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&ceid=2107230) _but still remain on our general email list!_

* **TOP NEWS**

"**EXCLUSIVE: Where is Congresswoman Kay Granger?** " Dallas Express' **Carlos Turcios** -- "Amid the ongoing drama in D.C. with Congress furiously debating and voting on an interim spending bill a key question has arisen from constituents in Texas Congressional District 12, "Where is Congresswoman **Kay Granger**?"

According to Ms. Granger's roll call [vote](https://substack.com/redirect/f958bb14-7ac3-4caf-8d45-6be2d133b9bc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) page, Granger's last vote was on July 24th, 2024 as she voted No to the "_Amendment in the House_ H.Amdt. 1157 (Miller) to H.R. 8998: To reduce the salary of Ya-Wei (Jake) Li, Deputy Assistant Administrator for Pesticide Programs, to $1."

Since then, Granger has not cast any votes in Washington on behalf of her Tarrant and Parker County constituents.

_The Dallas Express_ attempted to reach her district and D.C. offices but calls went directly to voicemail where a recorded message from Congresswoman Granger plays.

"I am sorry we are unable to answer your phone right now. We are really glad you called us. Please leave your name, phone number and a brief message and someone in our office will call you back as soon as possible," the recorded message says.

We then visited her office in person hoping to understand how Congresswoman Granger planned to vote on the continuing resolution this afternoon. Upon arrival, we found the door locked, front door glass window covered, no one inside, and no sign of the office continuing to be occupied.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/4d2095e5-c07c-43d2-a4e3-78138a9b2715?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
When we asked employees at the WestBend building where Ms. Granger's district office is located what happened to Ms. Granger's office we were told that her team had packed up and closed the office before Thanksgiving. Her office appears to be closed for good (she is retiring at the end of the session) with phone calls going unanswered and voice mails not returned.

We then received a tip from a Granger constituent who shared that the Congresswoman has been residing at a local memory care and assisted living home for some time after having been found wandering lost and confused in her former Cultural District/West 7th neighborhood.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/4d2095e5-c07c-43d2-a4e3-78138a9b2715?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
_The Dallas Express_ team visited the facility to confirm whether Granger was residing there and to inquire about how she planned to vote on the spending bill. Upon arrival, two employees confirmed that Granger is indeed living at the facility. However, we were not permitted to conduct an interview regarding the current spending debate in the House of Representatives and how or if Ms. Granger planned to vote.

**Taylor Manziel** who is the Assistant Executive Director for the senior living facility acknowledged to _The Dallas Express_ that "This is her home."" With a decline in the number of credible news organizations, the Must Read Texas morning email is indispensable for anyone that wants to continue to be informed." - Former U.S. Senator Phil Gramm (R-TX) AUSTIN NEWSLETTER LAUNCHED Are you one of the 100,000+ subscribers to , a newsletter that delivers EVERYTHING you NEED TO KNOW about Austin? | | ---|---|--- Subscriptions are $5/mo, $50/yr or $199/lifetime here: . » Read this morning's (free) edition . > SUBSCRIBE HERE: . ($5/mo or $50/yr or $199 lifetime) TOP NEWS "EXCLUSIVE: Where is Congresswoman Kay Granger? " Dallas Express' Carlos Turcios -- "Amid the ongoing drama in D.C. with Congress furiously debating and voting on an interim spending bill a key question has arisen from constituents in Texas Congressional District 12, "Where is Congresswoman Kay Granger?" According to Ms. Granger's roll call page, Granger's last vote was on July 24th, 2024 as she voted No to the "Amendment in the House H.Amdt. 1157 (Miller) to H.R. 8998: To reduce the salary of Ya-Wei (Jake) Li, Deputy Assistant Administrator for Pesticide Programs, to $1." Since then, Granger has not cast any votes in Washington on behalf of her Tarrant and Parker County constituents. The Dallas Express attempted to reach her district and D.C. offices but calls went directly to voicemail where a recorded message from Congresswoman Granger plays. "I am sorry we are unable to answer your phone right now. We are really glad you called us. Please leave your name, phone number and a brief message and someone in our office will call you back as soon as possible," the recorded message says. We then visited her office in person hoping to understand how Congresswoman Granger planned to vote on the continuing resolution this afternoon. Upon arrival, we found the door locked, front door glass window covered, no one inside, and no sign of the office continuing to be occupied. When we asked employees at the WestBend building where Ms. Granger's district office is located what happened to Ms. Granger's office we were told that her team had packed up and closed the office before Thanksgiving. Her office appears to be closed for good (she is retiring at the end of the session) with phone calls going unanswered and voice mails not returned. We then received a tip from a Granger constituent who shared that the Congresswoman has been residing at a local memory care and assisted living home for some time after having been found wandering lost and confused in her former Cultural District/West 7th neighborhood. The Dallas Express team visited the facility to confirm whether Granger was residing there and to inquire about how she planned to vote on the spending bill. Upon arrival, two employees confirmed that Granger is indeed living at the facility. However, we were not permitted to conduct an interview regarding the current spending debate in the House of Representatives and how or if Ms. Granger planned to vote. Taylor Manziel who is the Assistant Executive Director for the senior living facility acknowledged to The Dallas Express that "This is her home."" One of America's most Democratic cities continues to show us who the government really works for

# ["Our" government -- and an assassin returns!](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=153534357&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUzNTM0MzU3LCJpYXQiOjE3MzQ5NzUyODIsImV4cCI6MTczNzU2NzI4MiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE4Njc3NDEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.Opf0ayYZxFPT3awTddRVZmEFY0jjX2akeSsuwey9wCA)
### One of America's most Democratic cities continues to show us who the government really works for
| | [Lucas Kunce](https://substack.com/@lucaskunce)
---
| Dec 23
---
| [](https://substack.com/@lucaskunce)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=153534357&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUzNTM0MzU3LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTczNDk3NTI4MiwiZXhwIjoxNzM3NTY3MjgyLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTg2Nzc0MSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.eEfhYWBcEqbQWBTMq858jPucxA_p-IH29iDnHUlBuVA&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=153534357&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUzNTM0MzU3LCJpYXQiOjE3MzQ5NzUyODIsImV4cCI6MTczNzU2NzI4MiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE4Njc3NDEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.Opf0ayYZxFPT3awTddRVZmEFY0jjX2akeSsuwey9wCA&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=153534357&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUzNTM0MzU3LCJpYXQiOjE3MzQ5NzUyODIsImV4cCI6MTczNzU2NzI4MiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE4Njc3NDEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.Opf0ayYZxFPT3awTddRVZmEFY0jjX2akeSsuwey9wCA)

---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/lucaskunce/p/our-government-and-an-assassin-returns?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

On Saturday we talked about how the [President Musk situation](https://substack.com/redirect/103a344e-a508-4d90-b848-d879e3e16963?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is just the latest sign that America has devolved into oligarchy -- after the man single-handedly killed a widely accepted bipartisan bill because it would have put our interests (US national security) ahead of his interests (business investment in China).
A big thanks to everyone who shared the piece and commented and e-mailed. It was a great response and I hope you'll continue to share these with anyone you think might be interested!
Many are mocking Trump over this Musk situation, and joyfully pointing out that he's made himself a tool of the billionaire class here. But, other than giving you that momentary "I told you so" rush, I don't think it's going to help anything and it's certainly not going to give Democrats any sort of ground to take.
Yes, I do see the continued irony of people voting for Trump as if he's going to work for anyone but the billionaire class. But I also see the irony of people thinking that Democrats have a strong position on this.
After all, one of the most Democratic cities in the country just spent the last two weeks showing us who that government works for -- and it sure as hell isn't everyday people.
A couple of weeks ago I pointed out [what an assassin reveals about our politics](https://substack.com/redirect/25894017-8ab2-4c0a-98a7-4cfbab805a20?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and how the response to his act -- both by public officials and everyday people -- gave us an opportunity to look at one thing Democrats could do to flip the narrative. Namely, stop being company men like so many politicians were after the UnitedHealth murder -- wrap up your obligatory "murder is wrong" statement and start going after institutions that screw over everyday people for the elite class.
Since then, however, Democrats have instead used the situation to double down on showing the shareholder class how important they are compared to the rest of us.
In the wake of the UnitedHealth CEO's murder, New York Governor Kathy Hochul [proposed creating a special hotline](https://substack.com/redirect/1f60edae-9e1a-4a4d-929b-900c60670766?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for nervous CEOs to call if they felt unsafe. And to get input on the perks that this Amex Centurion exclusive 911 line should have, she held a massive meeting between her government team and 175 corporate representatives, state Homeland Security, counterterrorism officials, and private security teams.
The way we know about the meeting? Because Hochul _[went on MSNBC](https://substack.com/redirect/354d0ce0-8d10-4dd3-a2cc-e56709cadb90?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_ to outline her plan to dedicate more resources to making CEOs feel good.
As for the rest of us? Well, we still have regular 911, and I guess we should be thanking her because now we can also make funny TikTok videos pretending to be scared CEOs calling in to her concierge line. And we should also be grateful that more resources are being diverted from us in order to make the most heavily resourced people in the world feel warmer and fuzzier in their penthouses, estates, and luxury yachts. Because their comfort is certainly more important than anything we could want.
Hochul wasn't the only New York Democrat diverting resources to make CEOs feel safer. Let's turn to what another one was up to this week.
When the assassin, who we now know as Luigi Mangione, was extradited to New York he was escorted by more than two dozen NYPD officers, FBI agents, and, wildest of all, none other than the tough-talking (and [indicted](https://substack.com/redirect/cabe768c-0587-4df0-9c4e-0f3cc5537208?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)) Democratic Mayor of New York himself, [whose statement on his unique personal involvement](https://substack.com/redirect/7014d9d3-2659-4b50-904a-969f82e7c09f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in a single murder case says it all:
"I wanted to send a strong message with the police commissioner that we are leading from the front," Mayor Adams said during an interview. "I'm not going to just allow him to come into our city. I wanted to look him in the eye and state that, 'You carried out this terrorist act in my city, the city that the people of New York love.' And I wanted to be there to show the symbolism of that."
He delivered a strong message alright. But it wasn't for Luigi Mangione, or for any of us normal people. I mean, let's face it, are any of us worried in the slightest that Luigi Mangione is going to harm us? I don't think so.
In fact, if the Mayor wanted normal people to feel safer he would have pulled this stunt with the [guy who knifed three random people](https://substack.com/redirect/e7ba16a2-7b36-4cf5-a208-81e3c0e30b25?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to death in Manhattan a few weeks ago. But he didn't, did he?
Nope, instead the Mayor confronted Luigi Mangione with a message to the CEOs of New York, reminding them that they are special (they charged Luigi with terrorism, after all!) and that Democrat Eric Adams' government remains dedicated to them in every way possible.
Don't believe me yet? That these Democratic governments serve the billionaire class?
Well, at the same time, on the other side of town, NYPD officers were [literally locking arms to protect billionaire Jeff Bezos's supply lines](https://substack.com/redirect/63e8ef5d-4a94-44d5-9627-4c228d99b72a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from striking Amazon drivers who are tired of having to shit in bags on their delivery routes in order to meet his draconian quotas.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/66f80f23-4d64-46f1-9872-27f9ed45c166?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Bezos claims that his drivers, who wear Amazon emblazoned uniforms and drive Amazon trucks, aren't employees and so they can't organize and don't have employee rights or protections. And you know what? The law and the government might even be on his side because our government has been writing laws for guys like Bezos for a generation.
And if the law isn't exactly on his side? Our corporate judiciary, appointed by both parties, will make sure it is.
Our law and our government, at every level, have become tools to preserve the wealth of the shareholder class. Tools to help billionaires convert our wealth, our resources, our health, and labor into shareholder profits. It's obviously not just blue cities like New York. I also saw it several years ago when I was in Bessemer, Alabama, where the local police ran the same playbook for Amazon by intimidating workers trying to organize at the Amazon warehouse there.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/d7f18e27-2e70-4a0f-ab96-4382065cc153?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
But aren't Democrat-led governments supposed to be different? Isn't that why everyone is making fun of Trump and mocking him and the Republican Congress for letting Elon Musk call the shots? Aren't Democrats supposed to be for the little guy?
Actually, yes, they are. And one reason Democrats are getting crushed is because they don't have that anymore. I mean, Hochul intentionally went on MSNBC to excitedly brag about her proactive response to the concerns of CEOs -- but you'd be hard pressed to find clips of her talking about crime against everyday New Yorkers where she isn't totally on the defensive.
Just this brief look at the last two weeks in liberal New York, where the CEOs and Jeff Bezos are calling the shots, sure takes the wind out of the sails of any "I told you so's" around President Musk.
The American people are looking so hard for someone to change things. It's why they voted for Obama. And then why they voted for Trump. There could very well be a post-Trump opportunity for Democrats similar to the post-Obama opportunity for Republicans and the post-Bush one for Democrats before that. But not if they stay the course.
When Democrats serve as company men, and when the party governs for CEOs instead of workers, it really doesn't have anything to offer anymore.
As always, if you find this content engaging and interesting, please share it!

And if you're able to support this work financially so we can keep it up, [please consider a paid subscription.](https://substack.com/redirect/d49fcb38-37ec-4cad-8b5c-0f3c5aa46d2d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Until next time.
-- Lucas

[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9sdWNhc2t1bmNlLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.OncgW7hHYKk1GZY3fOj_TPFO-LdH1KpvTb4g1r1fpuY?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=postcta)

I mean, let's face it, are any of us worried in the slightest that Luigi Mangione is going to harm us? I don't think so. In fact, if the Mayor wanted normal people to feel safer he would have pulled this stunt with the to death in Manhattan a few weeks ago. But he didn't, did he? Nope, instead the Mayor confronted Luigi Mangione with a message to the CEOs of New York, reminding them that they are special (they charged Luigi with terrorism, after all!) and that Democrat Eric Adams' government remains dedicated to them in every way possible. Don't believe me yet? That these Democratic governments serve the billionaire class? Well, at the same time, on the other side of town, NYPD officers were from striking Amazon drivers who are tired of having to shit in bags on their delivery routes in order to meet his draconian quotas. | | ---|---|--- Bezos claims that his drivers, who wear Amazon emblazoned uniforms and drive Amazon trucks, aren't employees and so they can't organize and don't have employee rights or protections. And you know what? The law and the government might even be on his side because our government has been writing laws for guys like Bezos for a generation. And if the law isn't exactly on his side? Our corporate judiciary, appointed by both parties, will make sure it is. Our law and our government, at every level, have become tools to preserve the wealth of the shareholder class. Tools to help billionaires convert our wealth, our resources, our health, and labor into shareholder profits. It's obviously not just blue cities like New York. I also saw it several years ago when I was in Bessemer, Alabama, where the local police ran the same playbook for Amazon by intimidating workers trying to organize at the Amazon warehouse there. | | ---|---|--- But aren't Democrat-led governments supposed to be different? Isn't that why everyone is making fun of Trump and mocking him and the Republican Congress for letting Elon Musk call the shots? Aren't Democrats supposed to be for the little guy? Actually, yes, they are. And one reason Democrats are getting crushed is because they don't have that anymore. I mean, Hochul intentionally went on MSNBC to excitedly brag about her proactive response to the concerns of CEOs -- but you'd be hard pressed to find clips of her talking about crime against everyday New Yorkers where she isn't totally on the defensive. Just this brief look at the last two weeks in liberal New York, where the CEOs and Jeff Bezos are calling the shots, sure takes the wind out of the sails of any "I told you so's" around President Musk. The American people are looking so hard for someone to change things. It's why they voted for Obama. And then why they voted for Trump. There could very well be a post-Trump opportunity for Democrats similar to the post-Obama opportunity for Republicans and the post-Bush one for Democrats before that. But not if they stay the course. When Democrats serve as company men, and when the party governs for CEOs instead of workers, it really doesn't have anything to offer anymore. As always, if you find this content engaging and interesting, please share it! And if you're able to support this work financially so we can keep it up, Until next time. -- Lucas You're currently a free subscriber to . For the full experience, | | | | | | | (C) 2024 Lucas Kunce P.O. Box 1240, Independence, MO 64050 ? ?

* **TOP NEWS**

"**EXCLUSIVE: Where is Congresswoman Kay Granger?** " Dallas Express' **Carlos Turcios** -- "Amid the ongoing drama in D.C. with Congress furiously debating and voting on an interim spending bill a key question has arisen from constituents in Texas Congressional District 12, "Where is Congresswoman **Kay Granger**?"

According to Ms. Granger's roll call [vote](https://substack.com/redirect/2a540ba2-677f-43b3-8558-34996661cce0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) page, Granger's last vote was on July 24th, 2024 as she voted No to the "_Amendment in the House_ H.Amdt. 1157 (Miller) to H.R. 8998: To reduce the salary of Ya-Wei (Jake) Li, Deputy Assistant Administrator for Pesticide Programs, to $1."

Since then, Granger has not cast any votes in Washington on behalf of her Tarrant and Parker County constituents.

_The Dallas Express_ attempted to reach her district and D.C. offices but calls went directly to voicemail where a recorded message from Congresswoman Granger plays.

"I am sorry we are unable to answer your phone right now. We are really glad you called us. Please leave your name, phone number and a brief message and someone in our office will call you back as soon as possible," the recorded message says.

We then visited her office in person hoping to understand how Congresswoman Granger planned to vote on the continuing resolution this afternoon. Upon arrival, we found the door locked, front door glass window covered, no one inside, and no sign of the office continuing to be occupied.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/86a60c35-9807-47b2-bd34-0b4340f03563?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
When we asked employees at the WestBend building where Ms. Granger's district office is located what happened to Ms. Granger's office we were told that her team had packed up and closed the office before Thanksgiving. Her office appears to be closed for good (she is retiring at the end of the session) with phone calls going unanswered and voice mails not returned.

We then received a tip from a Granger constituent who shared that the Congresswoman has been residing at a local memory care and assisted living home for some time after having been found wandering lost and confused in her former Cultural District/West 7th neighborhood.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/86a60c35-9807-47b2-bd34-0b4340f03563?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
_The Dallas Express_ team visited the facility to confirm whether Granger was residing there and to inquire about how she planned to vote on the spending bill. Upon arrival, two employees confirmed that Granger is indeed living at the facility. However, we were not permitted to conduct an interview regarding the current spending debate in the House of Representatives and how or if Ms. Granger planned to vote.

**Taylor Manziel** who is the Assistant Executive Director for the senior living facility acknowledged to _The Dallas Express_ that "This is her home."" ($5/mo or $50/yr or $199 lifetime) TOP NEWS "EXCLUSIVE: Where is Congresswoman Kay Granger? " Dallas Express' Carlos Turcios -- "Amid the ongoing drama in D.C. with Congress furiously debating and voting on an interim spending bill a key question has arisen from constituents in Texas Congressional District 12, "Where is Congresswoman Kay Granger?" According to Ms. Granger's roll call page, Granger's last vote was on July 24th, 2024 as she voted No to the "Amendment in the House H.Amdt. 1157 (Miller) to H.R. 8998: To reduce the salary of Ya-Wei (Jake) Li, Deputy Assistant Administrator for Pesticide Programs, to $1." Since then, Granger has not cast any votes in Washington on behalf of her Tarrant and Parker County constituents. The Dallas Express attempted to reach her district and D.C. offices but calls went directly to voicemail where a recorded message from Congresswoman Granger plays. "I am sorry we are unable to answer your phone right now. We are really glad you called us. Please leave your name, phone number and a brief message and someone in our office will call you back as soon as possible," the recorded message says. We then visited her office in person hoping to understand how Congresswoman Granger planned to vote on the continuing resolution this afternoon. Upon arrival, we found the door locked, front door glass window covered, no one inside, and no sign of the office continuing to be occupied. When we asked employees at the WestBend building where Ms. Granger's district office is located what happened to Ms. Granger's office we were told that her team had packed up and closed the office before Thanksgiving. Her office appears to be closed for good (she is retiring at the end of the session) with phone calls going unanswered and voice mails not returned. We then received a tip from a Granger constituent who shared that the Congresswoman has been residing at a local memory care and assisted living home for some time after having been found wandering lost and confused in her former Cultural District/West 7th neighborhood. The Dallas Express team visited the facility to confirm whether Granger was residing there and to inquire about how she planned to vote on the spending bill. Upon arrival, two employees confirmed that Granger is indeed living at the facility. However, we were not permitted to conduct an interview regarding the current spending debate in the House of Representatives and how or if Ms. Granger planned to vote. Taylor Manziel who is the Assistant Executive Director for the senior living facility acknowledged to The Dallas Express that "This is her home."" name: Jazz Lewis
subject: Just a minute of your time
body:
https://www.jazzlewis.com/ [https://www.jazzlewis.com/]
Hi friend,
I’m sure you’re in the middle of holiday preparations, so I’ll be quick. We‘re just two weeks away from my “Meet the Whips” Happy Hour at Federal House on January 6, 2025 from 5-7 pm [http://bit.ly/JazzJan6] . I’ll be joined by Chief Deputy Whips Vaughn Stewart and Melissa Wells . We’ll celebrate the new year and new session where Vaughn, Melissa, and I can continue our dedication and commitment to making Maryland stronger, safer, and more equitable for all who live here.
As we say goodbye to 2024, I can promise that Melissa, Vaughn, and I are ready for anything that comes our way in the new year. Together, we’ll fight for what matters most to you and those you care about .
If you’d like to join us, click here [http://bit.ly/JazzJan6] or visit bit.ly/JazzJan6 [http://bit.ly/JazzJan6] . If you prefer to contribute by check or money order, mail your donation to Friends of Jazz Lewis, c/o Rice Consulting, 17 W Courtland St, Ste 210, Bel Air MD 21014.
Have a safe and joyous holiday,
Jazz
bit.ly/JazzJan6 [bit.ly/JazzJan6]
By Authority: Friends of Jazz Lewis, Chris Taitt, Treasurer
Friends of Jazz Lewis - State Account
P.O. Box 341854
Bethesda, MD 20827
United States

name: Jane Fonda
